trajectory_summary: The call builds genuine energy through the bill-swap pitch (peaking at 06:32 when Nancy paraphrases the concept back) but leaks all of it at the bill-collection wall. Holland never attempts to book an appointment — she dissolves the call into an open-ended 'text me when you have the bill' with no date, no time, and no commitment structure. The outcome is a soft callback with a warm lead, not a booked sit. LEAD-TYPE CAVEAT: the call's lead type is not identified in the transcript metadata. Per schema context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules, organic leads do not require the bill before booking. If this is an organic lead, Holland could have booked the appointment without the bill entirely — the gap is not the bill-collection path but the failure to book at all. If this is a paid lead, the bill was required and the correct move was a structured callback with a locked time, not an open-ended 'send it over.'
outcome_dispute: v1 tag is 'unknown' — the correct classification is 'soft_callback_no_anchor.' No appointment was booked. Nancy agreed to call Dominion on Monday and send the bill, but no date, time, or confirmation structure was established. This lead is at high no-show risk on the follow-up.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Booking-signal recognition and the deflect-then-book sequence
primary_coaching_focus.quote: So essentially, I'm swapping one bill for the other bill.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Nancy paraphrases the value proposition back at 06:32 — the clearest booking signal in the call — and Holland responds with more explanation. She then gets a second opening at 07:13 when she correctly deflects a closer-tier question but skips the booking ask that should follow. The call ends with no appointment. Holland can pitch (the bill-swap delivery at 03:51 is solid), but she does not recognize when the pitch has landed and does not know the next move. This is the single gap that cost the sit. LEAD-TYPE CAVEAT: if this is an organic lead, Holland did not need the bill to book at all — the gap is even starker. Manager should confirm lead type before the 1-on-1 and adjust the coaching frame accordingly.

key_moments.quote: it guarantees you savings within the first year you have solar as long as you have a credit score above 670. So would that qualify you?
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland states the threshold as 670 — schema-compliant. This is worth noting because SESSION18_fcbf33c1 shows a rep stating 650. Holland gets this right. The qualifier delivery is clean and Nancy confirms without friction.

key_moments.quote: K. Well, yeah, I can definitely get you set up to learn more. And I can kinda tell you the info I know. I'm just a scheduler, so I don't know too much.
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland's self-deprecation ('I'm just a scheduler, I don't know too much') is reactive hedging, not strategic positioning. The schema's universal escape hatch frames the setter's limited scope as a feature — 'I'm scheduling, they're solving' — which elevates the appointment. Holland's version does the opposite: it signals low authority before the pitch lands, which subtly devalues the meeting she is about to sell.

key_moments.quote: K. Well, yeah, I can definitely get you set up to learn more.
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland's bill-swap pitch at 03:51–04:14 is the call's best execution moment. She names the mechanism ('bill swap'), states the goal ('completely eliminate that bill and replace it with a cheaper solar bill'), and adds a credibility anchor ('if we can't save you money, we're not gonna try and sell it to you'). This pitch directly causes Nancy's peak engagement at 06:32. Holland should know she did this right.

key_moments.quote: we could try doing, like, a three way call with Dominion if you wanted to. Other than that, I'm not too sure how else you would be able to get that usage. Or if you wanted to call Dominion and get it, then you can just send it over to me.
key_moments.stewart_read: Nancy has no online account and no paper bill — a clear trigger for the verbal-readback pivot per schema bill_collection.alternative_paths.elderly_low_tech_protocol. Holland instead offers a three-way call with Dominion (high friction, uncertain outcome) and a 'call them yourself' option (defers the problem entirely). Neither path is the schema-recommended verbal readback. The lead-type caveat applies here: if this is an organic lead, Holland should have booked the appointment and deferred bill collection to the specialist site visit, bypassing this wall entirely.
